mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in non-farm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observed instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated unemployment rate above a critical threshold indicates persistent labor market slack and weak consumer confidence, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 45% of periods when UNRATE is above 9.0% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation is elevated while the federal funds rate remains very low, it signals an impending need for monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales reflect strong consumer confidence and spending, translating directly into higher corporate revenues and earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The average S&P 500 5-day return is negative when RSAFS 1-month percentage change exceeds 0.5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production indicates weakening manufacturing activity and overall economic output, leading to lower corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods when INDPRO 3-month average change is below -0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts act as a leading indicator of future economic activity and consumer confidence, stimulating related industries and contributing to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: A statistically significant negative correlation (p < 0.05) is observed between HOUST and S&P 500 20-day returns over a 7-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of sluggish GDP growth and persistently high unemployment signals a weak economic recovery or potential stagnation, which erodes corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when GDPC1 growth is below 2.0% and UNRATE is above 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate at or near the zero lower bound indicates highly accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital and encourages risk-taking, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The average S&P 500 5-day return is negative when FEDFUNDS is below 0.10% over any 5-year rolling window.
